beats.activity: Wake up, coffee, gentle stretches and breathing work at home.
beats.reasoning: Jack's established morning ritual; low energy carryover from yesterday (30/100) means he starts slow and grounded. Rain outside means he stays indoors for the routine.

beats.activity: Commute to First Hill studio, taking I-5 south to avoid SR-520 eastbound closure.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 eastbound is closed 9am–noon; Jack leaves early to avoid the snarl and arrive comfortably before his first class. I-5 is his reliable backup route.

beats.activity: Teach 8:30am yoga class at the studio.; One-on-one wellness coaching session with a client.
beats.location_name: First Hill Yoga Studio (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Jack's early morning class slot; steady rain outside fits the introspective, grounding energy of a Tuesday morning flow. His regulars will be there.

beats.activity: Break at studio; tea, light admin, camera gear check.
beats.location_name: First Hill Yoga Studio (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Buffer between classes; Jack stays put rather than venturing out in the rain. Feeds his habit of tending to his film camera and studio space.

beats.location_name: First Hill Yoga Studio (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Noon class; rain still steady. Jack stays in studio rather than splitting for lunch outside—pragmatic given weather and low energy.

beats.activity: Lunch: quick bowl at a neighborhood cafe near the studio.
beats.location_name: Voula's Offshore Grill & Lounge (First Hill, Seattle)

beats.location_name: First Hill Yoga Studio (First Hill, Seattle)
beats.reasoning: Rain ends by 2pm; Jack uses the tail end of the drizzle to finish coaching and prep. He's staying put at work rather than making errands.

beats.activity: Commute home to Eastlake; takes I-5 north, then surface streets.
beats.reasoning: SR-520 closure has lifted by now; Jack takes the familiar I-5 route home. Leaves studio early enough to beat evening traffic and recharge at home.

beats.activity: Rest and light painting in studio at home; skies clear, energy recovering.
beats.reasoning: Low energy from yesterday carries into evening; Jack honors that by staying home, painting, and letting the cleared skies and calm mood feed his creative work.

beats.activity: Simple dinner at home; reflection and journaling.
beats.reasoning: Jack winds down with a quiet meal and introspection rather than social plans. Fits his low-energy state and preference for close, intentional time.
Jack heads to bed early after a full teaching day and low-energy recovery. 10.75 hours of sleep allows him to recharge before Wednesday.

diary: Woke early to grey skies and that familiar Seattle drizzle. Went through my stretches and breathing work—needed it today, honestly. The commute was smooth enough; I'd checked the bridge closure alerts and took I-5 instead, so I dodged the worst of the cross-lake mess. Classes flowed well, even the 8:30am had good energy despite the weather. Grabbed tea between sessions and fiddled with my camera gear—been meaning to organize that. Lunch at Voula's was solid, nothing fancy, just what I needed. By afternoon the rain had stopped and the sky was breaking open. Came home early, painted a little in my studio while the light was actually decent. Made a simple dinner and journaled through some stuff that's been on my mind. There's something settling about a day like this—productive but unhurried. Feels like I'm moving at the right pace.
thought_process: I was cautious about the bridge closure from the start, so I preemptively planned my route—no point fighting traffic when I can work around it. The grey morning made me lean into the grounding work at home; I knew I'd need that centeredness before teaching. Once at the studio, it was just about showing up present for my clients and students. The midday classes kept me engaged, and I used my breaks wisely rather than scrolling. When the rain cleared and I got home, I felt genuinely lighter—the weather shift matched my mood. Painting instead of filling the afternoon with errands felt right. I'm learning to honor those intuitive pulls toward rest and creativity. Today didn't demand anything dramatic from me, and I didn't force it. That's the real win.
world_event_prompt: Grey, drizzly Tuesday with steady rain from 7am to 2pm, high of 58F. SR-520 bridge closed eastbound 9am-noon for emergency repair, snarling cross-lake traffic.
